assertions.evidence:
@TableTest("""
    Scenario           | Input | Money? appears in the valid-inputs test method

assertions.text: Null input is represented as a blank cell (not the string 'null') in the table
assertions.evidence: Null input         |       |   — blank cell in Input column, with explicit note: 'The blank cell in Input represents null (not the string "null")'

assertions.text: The exception cases (empty string, letters-only, negative) are handled — via a Throws? column, assertThrows in the method body, or a separate @TableTest — not silently omitted
assertions.evidence: Separate @TableTest rejectsInvalidInput covers all three: Empty string (''), Letters only (abc), Negative value (-5.00) using assertThrows(IllegalArgumentException.class, ...)

assertions.text: Valid inputs have a result column name ending with '?' (e.g. 'Money?' or 'Result?')
assertions.evidence: Column header 'Money?' in the valid-inputs table: Scenario | Input | Money?

assertions.text: Test method body contains no if or switch statements
assertions.evidence: parsesValidInput body: assertEquals(expected, Money.parse(input)); — rejectsInvalidInput body: assertThrows(IllegalArgumentException.class, () -> Money.parse(input)); — neither contains if or switch
